english: 1. Sch. reed, rush, flag, also = smyú-gu (smyu gu). — 2. Cs. sprout, the first shoot of corn etc., myú-gu sṅón-po (myu gu sngon po) Thgy. the young green corn.
1. to run, roam, stroll idle about Sch. — 2. to show, exhibit ostentatiously, to boast with Cs. v. dmyúg-pa (dmyug pa).

quick, swift, speedy, myúr-po (myur po) id. Mil.; mostly as adv., myúr-du (myur du) quickly, speedily; soon; ći-myúr (ci myur) as speedily as possible; myur-du-btsá-rtags (myur du btsa' rtags) symptoms of immediate parturition Med.

to examine closely, to search into, to scrutinize, c. accus. or termin. of place Stg., Mil., prob. but a different spelling for nyúl-ba (nyul ba). — lće-myúl (lce myul) Mṅg., Lt. a symptom of disease, acc. to Wise p. 282: a quivering motion of the tongue.

pf. myaṅs (myangs), also myoṅ (myong), fut. myaṅ (myang) W. *nyáṅ-će* (*nyang ce*), 1. to taste Dzl.; to try by tasting, myaṅ-bas źim-po tsor-nas (myang bas zhim po tshor nas) perceiving the relish by tasting; ro myóṅ-ba (ro myong ba) *ḍob-lág nyáṅ-će* (*dob lag nyang ce*) W., id.; to enjoy, mto-rís-kyi loṅs-spyód (mtho ris kyi longs spyod) the bliss of paradise Dzl.; myóṅ-bar byéd-pa (myong bar byed pa) to make, or to permit to, enjoy, kyod ćós-kyi zas myóṅ-bar byao (khyod chos kyi zas myong bar bya'o) I shall make thee enjoy the food of religious doctrine Sch., yet it may be rendered also more simply: thou wilt enjoy ... Dzl. 2v2, b. — 2. in philosophy: to perceive, in relation to the perceptions of sense, Ssk. वेदन. — 3. to experience, to suffer, both good and evil, sdug-bsṅal (sdug bsngal), distress etc. frq.; to get, mi-sdúg-pai lus (mi sdug pa'i lus) an ugly body; seldom with termin., ɣnás-skabs ɣźán-du myóṅ-bar ꜧgyúr-bai lás-rnams (gnas skabs gzhan du myong bar 'gyur ba'i las rnams) works which would bring upon their author another state of existence (after his death) Thgy.; myóṅ-bar mi ꜧgyúr-ba (myong bar mi 'gyur ba) to be preserved from Dom.; raṅ-gi byás-pa ráṅ-gi myóṅ-ba yin (rang gi byas pa rang gi myong ba yin) Pth. your own doings are your own sufferings; as you have brewed, so you must drink. — 4. auxil. of the pf. like byuṅ (byung), but chiefly in negative sentences: btsal ma myoṅ (btsal ma myong) Dzl. I have never yet sought, mtoṅ ma myoṅ (mthong ma myong) Mil. I have never yet seen, tos ma myoṅ (thos ma myong) Mil. I have never yet heard, — a construction, that has originated from the earlier one c. inf.: rdzun smrá-ba ma myoṅ (rdzun smra ba ma myong), dgé-bai semsskyéd-pa ma myoṅ (dge ba'i sems bskyed pa ma myong) dealing with falsehood, producing virtuous thoughts, has never happened to me yet Dzl.

to be low, dbus dma mta ɣnyis mtó-na (dbus dma' mtha' gnyis mtho na) if (in pregnancy) the middle parts of the body are low, and the sides high Med.; sbst. lowness; adj., also dmá-mo (dma' mo), low, low water, low voice, low rank, short measure or weight, frq.; dmá-la kyád-du gsód-pa (dma' la khyad du gsod pa) to despise the low and humble Lt.; dmá-na (dma' na) if I live in humble circumstances Dom.; ṅá-yis mto mto byás-pa dma dma byuṅ (nga yis mtho mtho byas pa dma dma byung) aspiring higher and higher, I fell deep Pth.; of religion: ćúṅ-zad dmá-bai dús-su (cung zad dma' ba'i dus su) as it had somewhat fallen into decay Pth.; dma ꜧbébs-pa (dma' 'bebs pa) (frq. written sma (sma)) W. *ma bab kál-će* (*ma bab kal ce*), and intrs. dma ꜧbáb-pa (dma' 'bab pa) to lower, to degrade, by words: to abuse, to vilify Do. by deeds: to deface, to deform, to mar Pth.; to disgrace, dishonour, profane Pth.; to humiliate Tar.; to oppress, to ruin Schr.; *ma-bab-ćan* (*ma bab can*) W. humiliated, brought low. — dmá-sa (dma' sa) 1. Sch. low land (?) 2. = dmán-sa (dman sa). — Cf. dmán-pa (dman pa).

Lexx. सेना 1. army, host, dmag-tsógs (dmag tshogs), dmag-dpúṅ (dmag dpung), less frq. dmag-ɣséb (dmag gseb) id.; dmag daṅ bćás-pa (dmag dang bcas pa) with an army Tar.; mí-la dmag skyúr-ba (mi la dmag skyur ba) to commit the command of an army to a person Glr.; yúl-la dmag ꜧdren-pa (yul la dmag 'dren pa) to lead an army against, to invade a country, frq.; dmag rgyúg-pa (dmag rgyug pa) Glr., *mag ṭáb-pa* (*mag tab pa*) C. to war, to make or wage war, dmag-rgyág (dmag rgyag) (or dmag-ꜧdrén (dmag 'dren)) res máṅ-du byéd-pa (res mang du byed pa) to make war upon each other Glr.; mú-stegs-pai dmág-gis bzuṅ (mu stegs pa'i dmag gis bzung) he was made a prisoner by an army of Brahmanists Glr.; dmag stoṅ (dmag stong) 1000 men Pth.; dmág-gi tsogs stoṅ-prág súm-ću (dmag gi tshogs stong phrag sum cu) an army of 30 000 men Dzl. — 2. in a gen. sense, multitude, number, host, *mag-líṅ(s)* (*mag ling(s)*) W. a beating up of game, a battue; *mag-nor* (*mag nor*) property of the community, = *(s)pí-nor* (*(s)pi nor*) W. — 3. Cs. and Sch. war. —
